scope.summary: Read-only audit of bead close evidence, plus a close template, consuming bead-close-evidence/v1.
in_scope: tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/ (the first three close documents, all the author's own)
out_of_scope: Retrofitting evidence onto the 334 pre-contract closes: the audit reports coverage rather than failing them.
Rewriting other agents' historical close reasons, including the 70 that carry an unreferenced live claim.
Wiring the audit into a CI workflow: no workflow YAML was changed.
Verifying that a cited CI job is green: that needs the network, and ci_taxonomy.py --status already owns it.

entry_points.name: scripts/check_bead_close_evidence.sh
entry_points.trace: Read-only audit over all 337 closed beads: 0 hard, 70 advisory. Exits non-zero only on hard findings.

entry_points.name: scripts/audit_bead_closes.py --template <bead-id>
entry_points.trace: Prints a close-evidence skeleton prefilled with HEAD and the real tree-clean state; used to produce the three documents in tests/artifacts/evidence/closes/.

limitations: readiness.basis is scoped-test, so this close cannot claim ready: the Required graph (clippy, workspace test) was NOT run. The diff contains zero Rust and the shared checkout held another pane's in-flight Rust edits, so a workspace run would have reported on their tree rather than this change.

source.tree_clean asserts that every file this close claims is committed at source.sha, verified with `git status --porcelain -- <scope.in_scope>`; it is not a claim that the shared working directory was pristine. See docs/BEAD_CLOSE_EVIDENCE.md.

The advisory tier is heuristic text-scanning over close reasons and is deliberately not gating: CITED_SHA_UNRESOLVABLE has a known legitimate trigger (etib.2 cites the upstream python-oracledb commit 6cfd00aa642e, which will never resolve in this repository).
Coverage is 3 of 337 closed beads. The audit does not fail on unevidenced closes by default, so it cannot force the backlog down; --strict exists for when it can.

The audit checks that a cited proof exists on disk and that its SHA matches source.sha, but does not open it to confirm it is itself green. A red required-proof would still satisfy PROOF_ARTIFACT_ABSENT.
